title: Hobbes Music 13th Birthday: Michelle Manetti ⥊ Mairi B b2b Hobbes + many more ⥊ Club_Nacht
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Join us this April as Hobbes Music marks 13 years in the game. We’re celebrating properly at The Bongo Club, with special guest and label alumnus Michelle Manetti.

DJ, vocalist, producer and promoter Michelle Manetti has deep roots in UK club culture. Cutting their teeth as an underage raver in the late 90s, they’ve since become a respected figure in East London’s queer underground, bringing over 20 years of experience behind the decks and a reputation built on consistency, taste and graft rather than hype.

A committed advocate for marginalised and under-represented artists, Michelle is the founder of the FLINTA*-focused party Fèmmme Fraîche at Dalston Superstore, a long-running bi-monthly celebrating 10 years this year, known for safe space, strong line-ups and a genuine sense of community. They’ve also held residencies at The Cause and Adonis, with regular appearances at Horse Meat Disco, Little Gay Brother, Homoelectric, Percolate and beyond.

More recently, Michelle has been working closely with Demi Riquisimo, with releases including the No Given Time EP and the 2023 standout “All I Need”, a track that’s done the rounds and will already feel familiar to many. If it’s not already in your listening pile, now’s the time to fix that. Track below! 

Michelle was also previously the vocalist in cult London trio The Off Key Hat, who released a slew of head-turning records via Andy Blake's legendary vinyl-only label, Dissident (late 00s), including the bona fide club hit, Emergency Calling. MANY moons later, a pair of stellar demos landed at Hobbes Music hq and the label released the killer double A-side single, Shot At Dawn b/w Warm Your Love, in 2018.  

Michelle last visited Bongo when they co-headlined the HM label's 5th birthday with Glasgow's adopted Slovenian star (and fellow HM-alumnus), Nightwave. Suffice to say, Michelle's closing set absolutely tore the roof off the joint and they went on to deliver the goods again the following night at Paradise Palms.  Suffice to say, we've been working on a return date for some time...

Support duties on the night come from HM label boss Hobbes, going back-to-back with Mairi B to mark the label anniversary in proper fashion. Making the trip up from Newcastle, Mairi is one half of label affiliates Accident Machine, as well as a fierce selector in her own right.

Additional support acts are still to be announced, with more names landing soon.

title: Temporal - Tre Reynolds
location: The Liquid Room, 9c Victoria Street; Edinburgh, EH1 2HE; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
For this chapter, we welcome Tre Reynolds, one of the UK’s most formidable rising talents. Tre represents the perfect intersection of House music’s heritage and its future, blending his roots in 90s rave culture with a modern, razor-sharp precision. Known for his infectious, groove-heavy percussion and signature vocal-led Tech House, Tre’s sound is built for the sweat-soaked walls of an institution like ours.
Closing the night is Dublin-based DJ and producer Danny P is rapidly emerging as one of Ireland’s most promising new talents, with his sound earning him attention across the country’s thriving underground scene and beyond. Danny made his mark with the release of his debut two-track EP with Finn Penny on Davies Records, before breaking through with “Jump Higher”, his collaboration with Riordan on TSZR, which has already
surpassed one million streams since its release in September 2025. The track’s success has cemented his reputation as a rising force in the new wave of Irish electronic artists.
With a growing list of unreleased collaborations gaining support from the likes of Obskür, Kettama, Chris Stussy, Robbie Doherty, Gaskin, Max Dean, Ellia Jaya, and Danny Howard, Danny’s productions are reaching dance floors around the world. Having already gained some key show supports with Riordan and Us Two, he continues to build serious momentum as he steps firmly into the spotlight as one of Dublin’s most
exciting new names in house music.
This isn't just about the headliners; it’s about the community. We are rounding out the night with a handpicked selection of local DJs and Selectors who have been instrumental in shaping the current Edinburgh scene. These artists will be digging deep to set the pace, ensuring the energy remains driving and distinct from the first beat to the last.

Expect a masterclass in the groove as we delve into the depths of your temporal lobe to bring the soul of modern House back to the forefront

title: Terminal V Festival 2026
location: Royal Highland Centre, Ingliston, Edinburgh, EH28 8NB
description:
Scotland's No. 1 electronic music festival, Terminal V Festival returns on April 18 + 19th 2026 with their much anticipated festival format…

Marking two epic days in the Scottish capital alongside 40,000 ravers and six fully immersive indoor and outdoor stages, the weekend is set to shake the foundations of Edinburgh's Royal Highland & Showground thanks to its diverse lineup which spans the spectrum of techno & beyond. Think new faces, fast rising talent and returning favourites.

Over 100 artists from every corner of the globe will be touching down in Edinburgh for the momentous occasion. Every stage will offer it's own standalone world, with bespoke architecture, mind-bending visuals and chest-rattling sound for a sensory experience built for those who live and breathe dance music.

Sara Landry brings the UK debut of her ETERNALISM show, her only Scottish appearance all year, while the likes of Klangkuenstler brings his unmistakable schranz sound back to the country for the first time since 2022 - a rare UK appearance.

Also joining the lineup is I Hate Models who marks a highly anticipated return alongside the likes of Clara Cuvé, Ben Hemsley, Robert Hood, Novah, Santøs, Patrick Mason, Vieze Asbak, East End Dubs, Patrick Topping, Mall Grab, Morgan Seatree, Klofama + MANY more.

Full day and stage splits have now been revealed for the festival. Each stage is designed with a clear purpose, allowing different parts of the electronic spectrum to exist without compromise, from peak-time headline techno to foundational house, from high-pressure intensity to emerging local voices.
 
Alongside a multi-faceted lineup and the addition of an extra 6th stage, a landmark £1 million investment into site development and production upgrades is in place for 2026. Following feedback, this is a transformative step designed to elevate every aspect of the festival experience. These improvements will roll out across all stages, facilities and audience areas and underpin Terminal V’s continued rise as one of Europe’s leading large-scale electronic music festivals. 

Consistently ranked among DJ Mag’s Best Festivals in the World, Terminal V continues to evolve at a pace matched only by the scene itself, which is why 40,000 people from all over the world travel in numbers to the festival.

-----------------------------
Strictly over 18s / Management reserve the right of admission / Subject to Licence / Full T&Cs

title: EPiKA: E2NMN
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
For EPiKA’s next chapter, we’re bringing E2NMN - a DJ and producer whose sound lives on the deeper, more immersive end of techno: atmospheric soundscapes, intricate rhythmic architecture, and powerful synth work that builds pressure without ever losing subtlety.

With appearances at heavyweight rooms including Tresor and OHM (Berlin), FVTVR (Paris) and many more, E2NMN has carved out a reputation for sets that feel like carefully-built journeys designed to pull you in and create a real connection on the dancefloor. Speaking about her approach, she talks of “encouraging introspection” while she plays, and that’s exactly the kind of energy we seek at EPiKA.

Now based in Berlin, she co-founded Warum nicht? Kollektiv, a label and event series rooted in the city’s underground spirit. As a producer, she’s been steadily cementing her sound with releases on Void Records, Headlight Recordings, NYXII (owned by Isabel Soto, who we hosted a couple of years ago), and more.

If you come to EPiKA for hypnotic momentum, shadowy atmosphere, and that feeling of forgetting there’s a world outside the dancefloor - this one is for you.

Support from EPiKA residents.

title: RIVAL CONSOLES
location: The Liquid Room, 9c Victoria Street; Edinburgh, EH1 2HE; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
One of the UK’s most influential creators of electronic music, Rival Consoles, returns with a brand new audio-visual live show, centred around his new album Landscape from Memory — marking a new chapter in an ongoing quest for refinement and evolution.

London-based producer Ryan Lee West, better known under the name Rival Consoles, is notable for making synthesisers sound human and atmospheric. Over the course of a critically acclaimed eighteen-year career, his music has diversified from the challenging electronic output of his early EPs, to gradually becoming more conceptual and metamorphic with his albums.

In July 2025 Rival Consoles released his latest studio album Landscape from Memory on Erased Tapes, and is presenting it with a brand new unique performance that will take the form of an immersive audio-visual experience in which he will present material from across the last 15 years of his career and beyond. From deep ambient works to his unique version of techno, and traversing the vast spectrum in between, the show will be a long-form narrative of ideas — many heard live for the first time — with visuals co-created and performed live by Sky Ainsbury.

“Impossible to ignore, and hard to forget” — Pitchfork “Ambient leaning, mind expanding electronics” — DJ Mag

This show is 14+ / Under 16's admitted with adult

title: NSA Day Culture - Jen Cardini + Logan Fisher
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Celebrating 31 years of NSA with

French-born Jen Cardini has been shaping dance floors for over three decades, from sweaty underground raves to Ibiza superclubs and offbeat warehouse parties. A regular at renowned clubs like Berghain, Bassiani, and Circoloco, and festivals including Glastonbury, Sonar, and Primavera, Cardini’s presence is both timeless and ever-evolving and is one of electronic music's true pioneer's.

Logan Fisher has been an important force in the UK's underground scene for over 30 years. Resident at the legendary Gardening Club in Covent Garden, London, where he ran the hedonistic Betty Ford Clinic, followed by curating the cult club night, Bumpy Capers at Plastic People and more recently the UK's hottest underground party Tryouts For The Human Race.

title: Potpourri: G Jones & EPROM
location: Sneaky Pete's, 73 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JW; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Potpourri invites their biggest headliners to date to the Sneaky Pete’s sweatbox. Edinburgh’s favourite experimental queer night welcomes G Jones & Eprom + Parkinson White to the booth. Potpourri’s High Beams, our custom club lighting rig and a signature hand-made incense scent will, of course, be there too!

When G Jones and Eprom collide, the result is a masterclass in sound design and raw energy. Touring in support of their latest collaborative work, Disk Utility, the duo fuses jungle, acid, and techno into a high-octane dismantling of the traditional DJ set. From the glitch-laden eruptions of "1000 Cuts" to the club-ready euphoria of "By Your Side," witness two of bass music’s most forward-thinking minds pushing the dance-floor into uncharted territory.

title: Max Cooper
location: Usher Hall, Edinburgh, Usher Hall, Lothian Road, Edinburgh, EH1 2EA
description:
Max Cooper is an electronic composer, multi-disciplinary artist, music label founder and former scientist who has carved out a unique space in music and visual art.

 

He holds a PhD in computational biology at the same time as being the first contemporary electronic musician to perform at the ancient Acropolis theatre in Athens and one of the first clutch of musicians to produce in Dolby Atmos.

 

Cooper has enquired for over 15 years through music, collaborations, and his label Mesh, to explore the intersections between the arts and sciences with installations, performances, immersive experiences, online media, music videos and live events. Anchored throughout is his emotive approach, connecting how ideas and forms feel, in a manner accessible to us all.

 

"I've always had a strong emotive bond with aesthetics, and found at an early age that the purified forms of electronic music carried a lot of weight for me, as did the reductive natural aesthetics of the sciences. That felt connection to both opened up a world I've been lost in ever since."


Pivoting from computational biology to electronic music might not seem like the most natural career transition, but for Cooper the commonalities are obvious:"In both fields, you're in a sense, free. Free from the limits of living systems, free from the limits of corporeal sound. The only constraints are in your mind."



Max Cooper has collaborated with Zaha Hadid Architects, Dolby, L-Acoustics, 4DSOUND, Barbican Centre, W1 Curates, The Babraham Institute and exhibited work at museums including the Pompidou Metz, ZKM Karlsruhe, Museum Wave Seoul and The Science Museum London.

title: Start Of Summer Shindig: OOFT! ⥊ Ann Tweak ⥊ Hobbes ⥊ Cosmico + more ⥊ Club_Nacht
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Summer officially starts here. On Saturday 20th June, ahead of the summer solstice, we’re pleased to welcome back our Start Of Summer Shindig, this time with a very special guest: OOFT!.

A true mainstay of Scotland’s underground for well over a decade, OOFT! (aka Ali Herron) has earned his reputation through consistently strong parties, releases and DJ sets rooted in house, disco, funk and Balearic sounds. A DJ first and foremost, his approach is musical unflashy, and forever in the know.... the kind of selector who knows how exactly to let a record breathe.

As a DJ he has held long-running residencies at some of Glasgow's top venues including theThe Sub Club, The Berkeley Suite and, more recently, weekly Sundays at La Cheetah for his KEEP ON residency with fellow Gtown veteran David Barbarossa. Beyond Glasgow, he’s toured extensively across the UK, Europe and further afield, bringing a sound that comfortably moves from slow-motion Balearic moods through underground disco to classic, soulful house.

In the studio, OOFT! has built a deep and varied catalogue, releasing on respected international labels and carving out a reputation for music that DJs actually play. His 2012 track 'Love & Soulsaving' became something of a modern underground classic moving effortlessly through DJ bags worldwide, clocking up close to three and a half million streams and selling out its vinyl run, copies of which now regularly fetch serious money second-hand.

Since the release of his debut album Intricacies Of Modern Life in 2018, the focus has shifted. While original productions continue, much of his studio time is now spent crafting bespoke edits, reworks and tweaks - tools designed purely for the dancefloor. Many are cut exclusively for his own sets, tested in the moment and never released publicly. It’s this behind-the-scenes graft - the quiet refinement of records you didn’t know you needed - that keeps OOFT! firmly in demand and endlessly trusted behind the decks.

⥊

title: Lone (Live) Hyperphantasia Tour - Edinburgh
location: Sneaky Pete's, 73 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JW; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
“Hyperphantasia is: Unhinged, unrestrained, self-indulgent bat-shit pop music from an alternate dimension. A beautiful, synthetic hall of mirrors head rush... Like taking every style I've worked with amping them up to 11 and firing them off a cliff”  - Lone 

Enter the kaleidoscopic world of Lone - returning to Greco-Roman for his first album in five years, ‘Hyperphantasia’.

An artist who has been soundtracking dancefloors since the early 2000s, Lone (real name Matt Cutler) made his production debut in 2008 with “Lemurian”, a hip-hop inspired release before moving into the vibrant future-facing soundscapes we have come to know. His back catalogue ranges through house, rave, ambient and electronica, and on ‘Hyperphantasia’, Cutler sets himself the challenge to bring all of those influences together for one body of work that he describes ‘like an album in my mind’. Referring back to the album title, the definition of hyperphantasia is a condition characterized by exceptionally vivid and detailed mental imagery and for this album he tested himself to see how close he could get the music to sound exactly like what he was hearing in his imagination.   

On Hyperphantasia, Lone deepens his relationship with vocals. Having previously relied on vocal samples or more abstract live vocal treatments, this latest album marks a shift toward richer, more pop-leaning sensibilities. Cutler makes a clear lyrical statement, enlisting a diverse and carefully chosen cast of collaborators: London-based artists and fellow Greco-Roman affiliates Ell Murphy and Lou Hayter, Barcelona’s breakthrough singer Bikôkô, cult Nottingham rapper Juga-Naut, and Hong Kong-born, London-based musician Merry Lamb Lamb. Together, they contribute to what stands as a career-defining project.

The end result is a cinematic experience exploding full of colour. You are introduced to the album with an old school rave anthem ‘Life Spark’ and an interlude welcoming you into this musical world. Like chapters in a novel, the album ebbs and flows beautifully between stripped-back melodies ‘Opening A Portal’, ‘Photographs That Don’t Exist’, ‘Sickly, Sweetly, Summer Movie’ and ‘Fruit Rots, Water Floats Downstream’, bubbling feel-good house ‘Affinity (Cloud Four Four Mix)’, ‘Triton’ and ‘ Wemove’, the rap-influenced ‘Throw The Ember’ and epic future-pop tracks ‘Miracle Mile’, ‘Big World’, ‘Scattergun’ and ‘Home’. The album ends with a full circle moment, back to the early hardcore and jungle rave scene, on ‘Ascenscion.png’.

title: Perel at discotia
location: Sneaky Pete's, 73 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JW; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
The disco/techno heat of Perel's releases on LCD Soundsystem's DFA label are matched by her genuinely thrilling DJ Sets. Drawing on her early love for brooding New Wave and Post-Punk, she's made seminal records on Running Back and Kompakt too. Berlin House Zenner, and closing sets at Sonar Barcelona and its Lisbon edition. She's known to play Koko and Berghain's Panorama Bar, and supported the Chemical Brothers.

For as long as she can remember, Annegret Fiedler has created electronic melodies to soundtrack her daydreams. Drawing on her early love for brooding New Wave and Post-Punk, she became Perel, an electronic producer, vocalist and DJ who spent much of the 2010s shaping her sound – and to just praise. As well as being the first German artist to sign to James Murphy’s seminal dance-punk label DFA Records, Perel has performed internationally as a live act and DJ, from the floors of throbbing techno clubs to major city festivals.

Growing up in the East German state of Saxony, she learned how to write and produce songs, inspired by Aphex Twin, Kraftwerk and The Cure, among others. Later drawn to Berlin and its underground club scene, she took on the persona of Perel, writing, producing and singing on solo material for labels like Running Back, Uncanny Valley, Kompakt and Permanent Vacation. In an ever-evolving club scene with myriad faces, Perel stood out by singing over her DJ sets, bridging the mood of her own live sound with her selections.

Perel’s live performances and DJ shows are a testament to her captivating stage presence and musical versatility. Highlights include performances at Outside Lands in San Francisco, her first hard ticket selling show in Berlin House Zenner, and closing sets at Sonar Barcelona and its Lisbon edition. She has also graced stages at Nation Of Gondwana, Fusion Festival, Koko, and the renowned Panorama Bar / Berghain, as well as support to the Chemical Brothers.

Now with a raft of new music out in 2025 and a recent move to Los Angeles she continues to evolve her timeless Neosynth to enjoy as the sun sets over the horizon and the freaks come out to play.

title: Feel The Real: 6th Borough Project, Frederick
location: People's Leisure Club, 45 Lothian Street, Edinburgh, EH1 1HB
description:
On Sunday 24th May, 6th Borough Project join Frederick for a special Bank Holiday session. This marks the first time in over 10 years that Craig Smith and The Revenge will play a back-to-back DJ set together in Edinburgh.

As The 6th Borough Project, Craig Smith and The Revenge have collaborated in the studio for more than 25 years, crafting quality house music while digging deep into long-forgotten funk and dusty disco dubs. Their releases on the revered Delusions Of Grandeur label, combined with their extended, genre-spanning back-to-back DJ sets, have firmly established them as trusted producers and selectors on dance floors worldwide.

Joining them is Frederick, an Edinburgh-based DJ and producer whose sound has been shaped through 15 years of close mentorship with Craig Smith. His ongoing collaboration with Osunalde has further informed his approach, grounding his music in rhythm, spirit, and depth.

The event takes place in Edinburgh’s newest basement venue — an intimate 200-capacity space in the city centre brought to life by Paradise Palms, Kelburn Arts, and The Gilded Balloon. Built for total immersion, it’s a room where the music takes centre stage.

Step onto one of the city’s most open and welcoming dance floors, where genuine music lovers come together. Secure your tickets and settle in for a Bank Holiday session of deep house jams, forgotten funk, and dusty disco dubs.

Set Times
6th Borough Project 19:00 - 23:00
Frederick 23:00 - 01:00

title: Musika x Skyline // Hernan Cattaneo // All-Dayer
location: TBA - Secret Location, null
description:
Musika presents Skyline's 4th Birthday on Sunday 24th May 2026, landing perfectly on the Bank Holiday weekend and shaping up to be one of the biggest nights Edinburgh has seen in years. To mark the occasion, we're bringing back a true legend. Hernan Cattaneo returns for his 7th Skyline appearance, delivering an extended 5‑hour set - and making his first Edinburgh performance in over two decades. A proper moment.

Joining him is Colombian superstar Kamilo Sanclemente, who blew everyone away last year with his UK debut: a mesmerising 4‑hour set supporting Grammy winners Deep Dish that had the entire room locked in from start to finish. With releases on Sudbeat, The Soundgarden, Anjunadeep and more, Kamilo has become one of the most respected names in modern progressive. This time he's back with a full 3‑hour journey, and Scotland is ready for it.

They'll be joined by the Skyline and Musika residents, along with a few special guests to round out the lineup and keep the energy flowing from doors to close.

To make things even smoother, return buses will run from Glasgow to the venue and back, bookable in advance - ideal for anyone making the Bank Holiday pilgrimage through for this one.

Sign up to Remind Me to get tickets before they sell out.

A Bank Holiday. A birthday. Two world‑class artists. A very special secret location to be announced. This isn't just a celebration - it's another milestone for the Scottish scene as East meets West following our mighty arches show last year.

Over 21+
